See also: [List of NRL records](/wiki/List_of_NRL_records "List of NRL records")

Official NRL statistics encompass all first grade competitions, namely the [New South Wales Rugby League](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Rugby_League_premiership "New South Wales Rugby League premiership"), [Australian Rugby League](/wiki/Australian_Rugby_League "Australian Rugby League"), [Super League](/wiki/Super_League_(Australia) "Super League (Australia)"), and the present day NRL.

### Team

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Rugby_League&action=edit&section=61 "Edit section: Team")]

* **Most premierships** – 21 [South Sydney Rabbitohs](/wiki/South_Sydney_Rabbitohs "South Sydney Rabbitohs")
* **Most minor premierships** – 20 [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters")
* **Most premierships in a row** – 11 [St George](/wiki/St._George_Dragons "St. George Dragons")
* **Most wooden spoons** – 17 [Western Suburbs Magpies](/wiki/Western_Suburbs_Magpies "Western Suburbs Magpies")
* **Highest score in a game** – 91, St. George against Canterbury (1935)
* **Largest winning margin in a game** – 85, St. George against Canterbury (1935)
* **Longest winning streak** – 19 matches, [Eastern Suburbs Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") (1975); Melbourne Storm ([2021](/wiki/2021_Melbourne_Storm_season "2021 Melbourne Storm season"))
* **Longest undefeated streak** – 35 matches, [Eastern Suburbs](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") (1935–1938)
* **Longest losing streak**- 42 matches, [University](/wiki/Sydney_Uni_Rugby_League_Club "Sydney Uni Rugby League Club") (1934–1936)

## Sponsorship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Rugby_League&action=edit&section=66 "Edit section: Sponsorship")]

The NRL and its clubs receive significant revenue from sponsorships, with sponsors' logos appearing on most parts of players' and referees' uniforms, the playing surface and even the ball itself. Since 2001, the National Rugby League premiership has been sponsored by [Telstra](/wiki/Telstra "Telstra") and known as the 'NRL Telstra Premiership'. Prior to this, the competition was simply known as the 'National Rugby League'.[[127]](#cite_note-129)

The Telstra Premiership has had five competition logos since 2001. The first, lasting only through the 2001 regular season, was the Telstra logo with an elongated circle enclosing the word *Premiership*. From the Finals series of 2001 through to the end of 2006 the logo was based around the shape of a football, with the words *Telstra Premiership* on respective lines along the bottom, culminating with a small football similar to the one in the official NRL logo at the peak. The main colours were dark blue and orange, the corporate colours of Telstra. The company worked with the NRL to create the third logo for the 2007 season onward as part of a new sponsorship deal. This logo was quite similar to the original National Rugby League emblem. From the 2011 Finals Series, a newer logo was commissioned in concert with a corporate re-brand undertaken by Telstra. The 2007 logo remained on club jerseys until 2012 due to the lateness of the change and was phased out in time for the 2013 NRL Season. From 2013, under a dramatic image rebranding of the competition, the Telstra Premiership logo was changed in conjunction with a new sponsorship deal to incorporate the key elements of rugby league in Australia to include the shield and chevrons, the green and gold, a red "Telstra" logo perched above the top of the shield and for the first time the iconic Provan-Summons trophy awarded to the premiers at the end of the season centred in the middle of the badge.

At the end of the 2018 season the NRL launched a bold new look for its Telstra Premiership. The new look aims to modernise the Telstra Premiership brand as well as integrate it more seamlessly with the 16 clubs. The new brand, highlighting the shield and the chevron – which have both been synonymous with Rugby League since the game's inception.[[128]](#cite_note-130)

Other notable sponsorships include [Kia](/wiki/Kia "Kia") (Thursday Night Football), [KFC](/wiki/KFC "KFC") (Friday Night Football), [Bundaberg Rum](/wiki/Bundaberg_Rum "Bundaberg Rum") (Super Saturday), [Chemist Warehouse](/wiki/Chemist_Warehouse "Chemist Warehouse") (Sunday Football). [Steeden](/wiki/Steeden "Steeden") is the official match ball supplier, [Westpac](/wiki/Westpac "Westpac") sponsor the match ball. The [State of Origin series](/wiki/State_of_Origin_series "State of Origin series") and the Australian [Test matches](/wiki/Test_match_(rugby_league) "Test match (rugby league)") (Kangaroos). [Youi](/wiki/Youi "Youi") and [Harvey Norman](/wiki/Harvey_Norman "Harvey Norman") sponsor the on-field match officials and Swyftx sponsors the NRL Bunker. Other Official NRL Sponsors are: [Coca-Cola Europacific Partners](/wiki/Coca-Cola_Europacific_Partners "Coca-Cola Europacific Partners"), [DoorDash](/wiki/DoorDash "DoorDash"), [Hankook](/wiki/Hankook "Hankook"), [Hisense](/wiki/Hisense "Hisense"), [Home Hardware](/wiki/Home_Hardware "Home Hardware"), [Rebel](/wiki/Rebel_(company) "Rebel (company)"), [Treasury Wine Estates](/wiki/Treasury_Wine_Estates "Treasury Wine Estates") ([Wolf Blass](/wiki/Wolf_Blass "Wolf Blass")), [Accor](/wiki/Accor "Accor"), [Drinkwise](/wiki/Drinkwise "Drinkwise"), [Red Bull](/wiki/Red_Bull "Red Bull"), [Carnival Cruise Line](/wiki/Carnival_Cruise_Line "Carnival Cruise Line"), [Gallagher](/wiki/Arthur_J_Gallagher_%26_Co "Arthur J Gallagher & Co") and EISS Super. Partners of the NRLW Competition are: Telstra, [Harvey Norman](/wiki/Harvey_Norman "Harvey Norman"), [Chemist Warehouse](/wiki/Chemist_Warehouse "Chemist Warehouse"), [Flight Centre](/wiki/Flight_Centre "Flight Centre") and Rebel.[[129]](#cite_note-131)

## Activism

### Same Sex Marriage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Rugby_League&action=edit&section=68 "Edit section: Same Sex Marriage")]

During the successful [Australian Marriage Law Postal Survey](/wiki/Australian_Marriage_Law_Postal_Survey "Australian Marriage Law Postal Survey"), National Rugby League supported the Yes vote.[[130]](#cite_note-nrl-132)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Rugby_League&action=edit&section=69 "Edit section: Voice to Parliament")]
